text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to managing projects, especially in software development. Work is divided into short phases called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint focuses on delivering a small, usable part of the final product. Teams hold brief daily standups, where each member shares what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any obstacles. The work itself comes from a list called the backlog, which contains user stories—simple descriptions of features from a customer’s perspective. After each sprint, the team reviews progress and adjusts priorities. This iterative development process allows teams to adapt quickly to changes and deliver value early.
